name: University Of Hawaii
description_short: University of Hawaii Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving the UH community with branch locations across Oahu.
description_long:
University of Hawaii Federal Credit Union is a federal credit union established to serve members of the University of Hawaii system and their families. As a credit union, it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The institution maintains multiple branch locations across Oahu, including its corporate office in Honolulu and branches at the McCully, Campus Center, Manoa Marketplace, and Kapolei Commons locations.

The credit union offers traditional financial services typical of NCUA-insured institutions, including member share accounts, share certificates, and various lending products. Members can access banking services through multiple conveniently located branches throughout Honolulu and Kapolei, with a centralized phone line for service inquiries. All locations operate under the same contact number, enabling streamlined member support across the institution.

University of Hawaii Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself through its community-focused mission serving the UH academic and administrative community. The strategic placement of branches, including locations directly on the UH campus (Campus Center Branch), reflects its commitment to accessibility for student and faculty members. As a not-for-profit institution, any earnings are returned to members rather than shareholders, potentially offering more favorable rates and lower fees than traditional banks.

The website content provided is limited to branch location information and does not detail specific loan products, interest rates, fee structures, or other service offerings. Prospective members should contact the credit union directly at (808) 983-5500 to obtain comprehensive information about membership eligibility requirements, account types, lending programs, and competitive pricing compared to alternative financial institutions.
services: Lending products for credit union members
In-person banking services at multiple branch locations
Multiple convenient branch locations across Oahu
pros: Multiple branch locations across Oahu for convenient in-person access
Campus Center Branch located directly on UH campus for student and faculty accessibility
NCUA-insured member accounts providing federal protection
Not-for-profit structure returns earnings to members rather than external shareholders
Dedicated phone line (808) 983-5500 for consistent member support across all branches
Serves specific community (University of Hawaii) with targeted financial services
cons: Limited geographic reach restricted to Hawaii locations only
Membership eligibility likely restricted to UH students, faculty, staff, and affiliated parties
Website provides minimal information about specific products, rates, or fees
No online account opening or digital banking details available from website
Limited service hours/details not specified on provided content
best_for: University of Hawaii students seeking convenient on-campus banking services
UH faculty and staff members wanting to bank with an institution serving their community
Hawaii residents affiliated with the University of Hawaii system
Members seeking NCUA-insured accounts with not-for-profit member-owned structure
diagnosis: University of Hawaii Federal Credit Union is best for current and affiliated members of the University of Hawaii system seeking NCUA-insured banking with a not-for-profit structure and convenient Oahu-based branch access. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is likely restricted to the UH community, and the website provides insufficient detail about specific products, rates, and services to conduct a full competitive analysis.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
